Tier 2 5.4-40 Revision 55.4.5.5 Pressurizer Materials
The pressurizer includes the top portion of  the RPV upper shell, the RPV upper head, 
the pressurizer baffle plate, heater bundles, and spray nozzles. The material of the RPV upper shell and upper head is described in  Section 5.2. The pressurizer spray nozzles 
and pressurizer spray nozzle safe ends are described in Section 4.5 and Section 5.2, 
respectively. 
The pressurizer baffle plate is an integral part of one of the RP V upper shell forgings 
and is thus constructed of a Grade 3, Class 2, low alloy steel in acco rdance with material 
specification SA-508 of BPVC, Section II. The upper and lower  surfaces of the baffle 
plate are weld clad with two layers of st ainless steel for corrosion protection in 
accordance with material specification SFA- 5.9 of BPVC, Section II; the first layer being 
Alloy Type ER309L and the second layer being Alloy Type ER308L. Additionally, the 
upper surface of the baffle plate in the regi ons of the SG integral steam plenums is clad 
with nickel-based Alloy 52/152 for compatibility.
The specific materials for the heater bundle assemblies are austenitic stainless steel or 
Ni-Cr-Fe Alloy. Materials for the cover plate, bolting, and heater sheaths are corrosion 
resistant materials that are compatible with  their respective operating environments. 
Materials for the cover plate include austenit ic stainless steel Type 304, Grade F304 and 
nickel-based Alloy 690 (UNS N06690). These ma terials comply with BPVC, Section II, 
Specifications SA-182 and SB-168, respectively . Materials for the heater sheaths include 
austenitic stainless steel Type 304L and Ty pe 316. These materials comply with BPVC, 
Section II, Specification SA-213. The bolt ing material is nickel-based Alloy 750 
(UNS N07718) and complies with BPVC , Section II, Specification SB-637.

nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting, all chosen for their corrosion resistance and compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications.

edges.description: Section A provides a narrative system description of RCS functions and components, while Section B offers the corresponding drawings and diagrams that visually clarify and detail the components and instrumentation referenced in Section A.

Section A provides specific details about the RCS's degasification and pressure-retaining functions, which are part of the broader plant systems and operating characteristics generally described in Section B.

Section A describes specific operational features and functions of the RCS, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and identifies which RCS components are included in the certified design, connecting them by specifying which described features are within the regulatory scope.

Section A describes specific RCS features (e.g., degasification, pressure boundary integrity) that are evaluated in Section B when establishing and optimizing steady-state thermal-hydraulic operating conditions for the NuScale RCS.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures, including piping and instrumentation diagrams, that visually clarify and specify the general plant design and system descriptions summarized in Section B.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures, such as piping and instrumentation diagrams, that visually clarify the design information referenced in Section B, which outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and site-specific design.

Section A provides the detailed drawings and diagrams, such as the RCS flow diagram (Figure 5.1-3), which are referenced in Section B to support the evaluation and description of the thermal-hydraulic operating conditions of the NuScale RCS.

Section A provides a general description of the plant design, including identification of systems and components, while Section B specifies the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design (as described in Section A) and the site-specific design for the COL application.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's systems, including the RCS, while Section B specifies the evaluation and optimization of the RCS's thermal-hydraulic parameters, thus detailing aspects introduced in Section A.

Section A identifies the certified design boundaries and components, which establishes the scope for the system evaluation in Section B that analyzes the thermal-hydraulic operating conditions of those components.

Section A describes the testing methods and conditions used to evaluate steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance, which provides supporting data for the system-level functions and operational descriptions outlined in Section B regarding the RCS's ability to maintain pressure and remove noncondensible gases.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic testing methods and results for steam generator tubes, while Section B provides the piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify the system layout referenced in the analysis of RCS operating conditions, connecting the test data to the physical system configuration.

Section A provides specific details about the steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance testing, which supports and elaborates on the general plant description and operating characteristics outlined in Section B.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and conformance requirements for the RCS and main steam system pressures, while Section B describes the physical features and operational functions of the RCS that are relevant to meeting those criteria.

Section A references the regulatory criteria and analytical parameters for evaluating core damage potential and system pressures, while Section B provides the detailed drawings and diagrams (such as P&IDs) that visually clarify the design information needed to understand and analyze those criteria and parameters.

Section A references specific regulatory criteria and analytical parameters for evaluating core damage and system pressures, which are based on or require the general plant design and operating characteristics described in Section B.

Section A provides critical heat flux testing data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary inputs to accurately characterize the thermal-hydraulic operating conditions described in Section B's system overview of the RCS.

Section A provides critical heat flux test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which inform the thermal-hydraulic parameters that are visually represented in Section B's piping and instrumentation diagrams, connecting experimental performance data to system-level design documentation.
Section A provides specific thermal-hydraulic test data and fuel performance correlations that support the general plant operating characteristics described in Section B.

Section A specifies regulatory criteria and analytical parameters for evaluating RCS and main steam system pressures during various operational and accident scenarios, while Section B provides a system description of the RCS components and their functions, which are necessary to understand and assess compliance with those criteria.

Section A references the need for specific analytical parameters and criteria (such as RCS pressure) that are visually clarified and detailed through the piping and instrumentation diagrams described in Section B, connecting regulatory requirements to system design documentation.

Section A references specific analytical parameters and regulatory criteria for the reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, which are general system characteristics described in Section B's overall plant description.

Section A describes the Decay Heat Removal System's function to remove decay heat from the RCS under specific conditions, while Section B outlines the RCS's design features that support normal and emergency operations, connecting them through the operational context and safety functions of the RCS.

Section A describes the functional design and operational purpose of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS), while Section B provides the supporting piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify the system's configuration and components referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the specific function and design basis of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS), which is one of the engineered safety features generally listed in Section B's overall plant description.

Section A specifies the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is a subsystem described in Section B's general overview of the RCS and its functions, connecting the detailed design elements to the broader system description.

Section A references specific sections and components whose detailed design and material information are visually clarified by the drawings and diagrams described in Section B, establishing a cross-reference between textual descriptions and visual representations.

Section A provides detailed component and material information for the pressurizer, which is one of the systems generally described in Section B's overview of the plant's systems and operating characteristics.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the steam generator, which provides empirical data relevant to the operating conditions of the RCS, while Section B outlines the regulatory criteria and analytical parameters (such as pressure) that these operating conditions must satisfy.

Section A describes the methodology for steam generator thermal-hydraulic testing using fluid-heated tubes with controlled heat fluxes, while Section B details a related test program focused on obtaining critical heat flux data for the NuScale fuel design, both contributing experimental data to validate thermal-hydraulic performance correlations for the RCS.

Section A provides detailed test data on steam generator thermal-hydraulic performance under controlled conditions, which supports the assumptions and analytical parameters regarding reactor coolant and main steam system pressures described in Section B.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic testing methods and heat flux conditions relevant to the steam generator, which provide data and boundary conditions that inform the design and operation of the Decay Heat Removal System discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic testing methodology for steam generator tubes, while Section B details the design and materials of the pressurizer, both providing specific technical information that collectively supports a comprehensive understanding of the NuScale RCS thermal-hydraulic operating conditions as required by the analysis question.

Section B provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which supports Section A's evaluation of core damage potential and conformance with regulatory criteria by supplying necessary thermal-hydraulic performance evidence.
Section B provides the initial power level and plant operating mode assumptions that are prerequisites for the evaluation of core damage potential and analytical parameters discussed in Section A.

Section B describes the design basis and function of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS), which is referenced in Section A as a system whose inadvertent operation is analyzed for its impact on thermal-hydraulic conditions in the RCS, thus connecting the system's design (B) to its role in accident analysis (A).

Section A references regulatory criteria and conformance for the reactor coolant and main steam system pressure, while Section B provides detailed design information about the pressurizer materials and components that are part of the Reactor Coolant System, thus Section B specifies the technical details underlying the general regulatory requirements discussed in Section A.

Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary to validate the thermal-hydraulic performance assumptions and analytical parameters referenced in Section B's regulatory evaluation of RCS and steam system operating conditions.

Section A provides critical heat flux (CHF) data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary to establish the fuel design limits that Section B's Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) must not exceed during post-trip cooling to ensure safe shutdown.

Section A provides critical heat flux testing data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which are necessary inputs for determining the thermal-hydraulic operating conditions of the RCS described in Section B's component and subsystem design.

Section A references the analytical parameters and criteria for evaluating steam system piping failures, including the operation of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS), while Section B specifies the design basis and function of the DHRS in removing decay heat and ensuring safe shutdown, thus Section B provides detailed system design information that supports the general analytical assumptions and criteria in Section A.

Section A specifies the regulatory criteria and analytical parameters for evaluating the reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, which are directly influenced by the design and material details of the pressurizer components described in Section B.

Section A describes the function and design basis of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) in maintaining safe thermal-hydraulic conditions in the RCS, while Section B provides material specifications for the pressurizer, a component within the RCS, thus Section B specifies details for a component referenced in the broader system context of Section A.
